rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE KING et al VS. AC AND S,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 16. DEFENDANT MAREMONT CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of production based on answers to defendant's special interrogatories and deposition testimony. Plaintiff has not submitted evidence that creates a triable issue of fact, specifically plaintiff fails to provide evidence that decedent was exposed to any of defendant's products that witnesses were not questioned on in the depositions. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MINAKO TOGASHI VS. CHRISTINE ELI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Christine Eli, Sammy Johnson And Zena Psychic Clairvoyant, Llc Motion To Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Outcome Of Parallel Criminl Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 2. Defendants Christine Eli, Sammy Johnson and Zena Psychic Clairvoyant, L L C'S Motion to Stay Civil Proceedings Pending Outcome of Parallel Criminal Prosecution. The motion to stay is granted for 6 months. The parties are to appear in the department on December 15, 2008 to address the issue of the stay. If the criminal case is resolved before then, the parties may appear on ex parte calendar to lift the stay. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ZETTNER et al VS. BUFFALO PUMPS INC(SUED INDIVIDULLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 17. DEFENDANT BUFFALO PUMPS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to sustain burden of proof on the issue of original or replacement parts. Special interrogatories 6 b and 6 c fail to require plaintiff to distinguish between the two. If Admiral Horne's declaration had been presented in the moving papers, the Court could consider it; it cannot do so when the declaration is filed with the reply. The Court declines to adopt the duty to warn theory as to replacement parts. Summary adjudication is granted as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action and punitive damages. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES R BANCROFT VS. EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION,A TAX-EXEMPT CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is denied. At this stage, the Court is unable to determine that the allegations of the relationship between ESC and the Diocese are either false or irrelevant, albeit perhaps tenuous. Plaintiff is entitled to attempt to prove that the contract is ambiguous. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES R BANCROFT VS. EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION,A TAX-EXEMPT CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT EPISCOPAL SENIOR COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the 6th cause of action, breach of fiduciary duty. The existence of the relationship is a question of fact and plaintiff has alleged the elements described in Richelle v. Roman Catholic Archbishop (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 257, 272, fn. 6. The demurrer is sustained as to the 7th cause of action without leave to amend. It is repetitive and unnecessary in light of plaintiff's breach of contract cause of action. Moreover, plaintiff's claim has matured and there is no need for declaratory relief. The demurrer is sustained as to the 11th cause of action, third party beneficiary. Plaintiff is a direct beneficiary of the life care contract and is not, as a matter of law, a third party beneficiary of the Episcopal canons as alleged in paragraph 154. The demurrer to the 13th cause of action for an accounting is overruled based on the Court's ruling on the 6th cause of action. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES R BANCROFT VS. EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION,A TAX-EXEMPT CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT JTM COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 12th cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that these defendants are agents of defendant EHF and acquired an identifiable sum of money that was take, in whole or part, fraudulently from plaintiff. The allegations are sufficient for the purpose of pleading. The demurrer to the 13th cause of action for an accounting is overruled given the Court's ruling on the 12th cause of action. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES R BANCROFT VS. EPISCOPAL HOMES FOUNDATION,A TAX-EXEMPT CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT LYTTON GARDENS SENIOR COMMUNITIES' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA SCHAEFER et al VS. WILBUR WOO INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltfs Motion Seeking Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA SCHAEFER and JEFFREY CRANDALL'S Motion Seeking Leave To Amend is denied. The alleged complaint in paragraphs 25 and 26 makes clear the alleged wrongs began in 2004, that the statute of limitations has run on the F E H A claims, that the other alleged causes of action - 15, 16, 17 and 18 - are subject to demurrer for failure to plead sufficient facts. The facts alleged also fail to demonstrate that Schaeffer is a member of any protected. class. Further, this amendment is brought on the eve of the filing of summary judgment motions and the amendment could therefore prejudice the ability of the parties to address all of the issues. The facts allegedly recently discovered come within the scope of plaintiff's original complaint. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: SF WHARF ENTERPRISES, INC., FORMERLY, DBA SAN VS. W.W. WHARF GL, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Pursuant To Cccp 416.5(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT JEFFREY LOWENTHAL'S Motion For Attorney's Fees And Costs Pursuant To CCP 416.5(C). The motion for attorneys fees is granted and the motion for costs is denied. W. W. Wharf is awarded $23,300.00; Lowenthal is awarded $17,000.00. The Court finds the hourly rates to be reasonable and that work on the demurrer and anti-SLAPP motion were intertwined and supportive of the anti-SLAPP issues. These defendants had a common interest and made identical arguments. The total hours sought, 135.5, is excessive. The Court exercises its discretion and reduces the hours by 25%. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SF WHARF ENTERPRISES, INC., FORMERLY, DBA SAN VS. W.W. WHARF GL, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Atty'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT W.W. WHARF G L, INC Motion For Attorney's Fees. The motion for attorneys fees is granted and the motion for costs is denied. W. W. Wharf is awarded $23,300.00; Lowenthal is awarded $17,000.00. The Court finds the hourly rates to be reasonable and that work on the demurrer and anti-SLAPP motion were intertwined and supportive of the anti-SLAPP issues. These defendants had a common interest and made identical arguments. The total hours sought, 135.5, is excessive. The Court exercises its discretion and reduces the hours by 25%. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: COUNTY OF ALAMEDA et al VS. JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 12. PLAINTIFFS COUNTY OF ALAMEDA and ALAMEDA COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE'S Motion To Tax Costs. The motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant is not entitled to recover the following costs: mediation expenses, courrier costs and videotape synchronization service. Defendant is entitled to recover the following costs: Depositions, $18,208.23; service of process, $595.00; witness fees, $835.55, totalling $19,638.78. Judgment in favor of defendants for costs is stayed pending the outcome of plaintiff's appeal. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA UNG VS. VICENTE A SOTO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT VICENTE SOTO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Denied. The stipulation for settlement was in english and signed by defendant. This is persuasive evidence that defendant had a sufficient understanding of english and understood that plaintiff was seeking judgment. Defendant has failed to show he has any defense to plaintiff's claim. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JACK J. WOLF VS. JAMES L. BROWN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 14, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANTS JAMES BROWN and ROBERT CARNAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 60 day notices for the complaint was filed before the 60 day period expired. Sustained as to the 3 day notice re: physical assault to cure or quit without leave to amend. Plaintiff's notice should be simply one to quit. Sustain with leave to amend as the notice to pay rent or quit or cure or quit re: storage for failure to attach the lease. =(302/PJM/AY)
